firstSection.title: Bridging Backend Engineering and Frontend Experiences
firstSection.text:
Prior to 2016, I worked as a systems engineer.
I enjoyed approaching technical challenges as if solving puzzles.

However, focusing solely on the backend made me increasingly aware of design-related issues.
I wanted to contribute not only to the core system but also to the user experience and visual design.
I transitioned to another web studio in 2016.

As the sole engineer on many web production projects, I handled a wide range of responsibilities, including WordPress customization, technical support, and workflow optimization.

For development projects, I built websites from scratch based on design mockups created in Adobe Illustrator.
During the maintenance phase, I implemented changes per instruction sheets, often implementing updates even when no design assets were provided.

Outside of work, I enjoy creating personal artwork and exploring independent publications and zines for creative inspiration.

I want to continue developing my skills in both backend and frontend development with my comprehensive experience.
I hope to continue working on projects that I find genuinely enjoyable and fulfilling over the long term.

data.title: TOEIC 440
data.description:
Taken at a time when my hearing difficulties and developmental disorders were undiagnosed.
Reading comprehension for explicit information, understanding specifications, and fact-based English text processing were stable without accommodations.

data.title: Practical English Proficiency Test (Eiken), Grade 2
data.description:
At that time, I learned at the Kumon Higher Education Division for speed reading.
Due to the drawbacks of language comprehension, I struggled with the listening and interview sections, leading to a decision not to retake the exam.

data.title: C Language Programming Capability Recognition Test - Grade 2
Japanese Riddle-Solving Certification (Nazoken), Grade 7
data.description: Scored average, ranking 1,552nd out of 3,711 nationwide participants in 'Reasoning Ability.'

data.title: Data Science Mathematics Strategist - Intermediate Level

VisualThinking.text:
As part of researching my family background, I published an independent research article on the “MIXED-RACE ORPHAN COMMUNITY AND ITS CULTURAL EVOLUTION” in 1970s Nagoya, a movement in which my grandparents were involved.
Given the sensitive nature of the topic, I focused on staying objective and looking at it from multiple perspectives.
For this research to be accurate, I gathered a wide range of sources, from social media, old books, newspapers, and academic papers.
This experience taught me to view issues through the lens of social systems rather than as isolated incidents.

The ability to organize information and think in terms of systems directly translates to my current work in web development.
I routinely consult official English documentation from Google and tech communities to ensure that website maintenance and optimization are based on accurate information.

I like expressing feelings and ideas through design, and as a hobby, I have engaged in personal projects such as illustration, image processing, and video editing.
Moving forward, I look to continue exploring these forms of expression.
